payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Children claim completed tasks for pocket money
payload.justification: Claiming tasks is a core process the enterprise performs to enable the pocket money system; explicitly described as the core purpose.

payload.description: Parents define tasks and assign monetary rewards
payload.justification: Task definition and reward assignment is a strategic process performed by the enterprise to establish work opportunities.

payload.description: Parents review earnings and tasks for payment fulfillment
payload.justification: Parental review and payment fulfillment is a strategic oversight process ensuring system integrity and completion.

payload.description: Provide visibility of available and completed tasks to children
payload.justification: Visibility provision is a strategic capability the enterprise must perform to enable child engagement.

payload.description: Provide weekly earning visibility for motivational feedback
payload.justification: Motivational feedback through earnings visibility is a strategic function supporting behavioral goals.

payload.description: Retain historical records for accountability and trend analysis
payload.justification: Record retention is a strategic function supporting long-term accountability and analytical capabilities.

payload.description: Task as a strategic unit of work assigned to children
payload.justification: Tasks are the core entity enabling the pocket money system; referenced across multiple signals as things that are claimed, defined, viewed, and completed.

payload.description: Monetary reward associated with task completion
payload.justification: Monetary rewards are things of strategic importance that define the value exchange in the system; explicitly mentioned as associated with tasks.

payload.description: Weekly earnings as aggregated compensation record
payload.justification: Weekly earnings represent a strategic data entity providing motivational feedback; distinct from individual task rewards.

payload.description: Historical record of tasks and earnings
payload.justification: Historical records are strategic information assets retained for accountability and trend analysis purposes.

payload.description: Task completion status relationship
payload.justification: The relationship between tasks and their completion state is strategically important for distinguishing available from completed work.

payload.description: Weekly cycle for earnings aggregation and visibility
payload.justification: The weekly cycle is a strategic time boundary for measuring and presenting earnings to provide motivational feedback.

payload.description: Task completion event triggering claim eligibility
payload.justification: Task completion is a strategic state change event that enables claiming and affects task visibility status.

payload.description: Child as task claimant and earner
payload.justification: Children are strategic stakeholders who claim tasks, view opportunities, and receive earnings feedback.

payload.description: Parent as task definer and payment fulfiller
payload.justification: Parents are strategic stakeholders who define work, set rewards, review outcomes, and fulfill payment obligations.

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: This is the core strategic goal of the enterprise expressed as the primary purpose of the system.

payload.description: Motivate children through visible earnings feedback
payload.justification: Motivation through earnings visibility is explicitly stated as a strategic goal driving the feedback mechanism.

payload.description: Ensure accountability through historical record retention
payload.justification: Accountability is identified as a strategic driver for maintaining historical data.

payload.description: Support trend analysis through historical data
payload.justification: Trend analysis capability is a strategic goal driving the retention of historical records.

payload.description: Claiming completed tasks for pocket money tracking
payload.justification: SG922 identifies the core business process of children claiming tasks to track pocket money.

payload.description: Defining tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: SG923 describes the business function performed by parents to establish task inventory.

payload.description: Presenting weekly earnings to child
payload.justification: SG926 defines the business function of aggregating and presenting earnings at week end.

payload.description: Viewing available and completed tasks, accessing child earnings and completion reports
payload.justification: SG925 specifies the child stakeholder capability to access task visibility information. | SG927 specifies parent stakeholder capability to retrieve payment fulfillment data.

payload.description: Weekly task lifecycle reset and availability renewal
payload.justification: SG928 establishes the business rule governing task lifecycle behavior on weekly boundaries.

payload.description: Historical task completion data must be retained
payload.justification: SG929 specifies the business rule requiring persistent retention of completion records.

payload.description: Child business stakeholder
payload.justification: SG922 and SG925 clearly identify children as core business entities who claim tasks and view completion data.

payload.description: Parent business stakeholder
payload.justification: SG923 and SG927 establish parents as business entities who define tasks and access earnings reports.

payload.description: Task business object
payload.justification: Tasks are the central business entity referenced across multiple signals as objects defined, claimed, completed, and reset.

payload.description: Monetary value associated with task
payload.justification: SG923 explicitly defines monetary values as attributes of tasks established by parents.

payload.description: Task completion status
payload.justification: Task completion state is an attribute tracked across multiple signals for viewing and historical retention.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregate
payload.justification: SG926 and SG927 specify weekly earnings as aggregated business data attributes presented to stakeholders.

payload.description: Parent defines task with monetary value
payload.justification: SG923 establishes the business relationship where parents create and value tasks.

payload.description: Child completes task
payload.justification: SG924 defines the conceptual association linking completed tasks to the child performer.

payload.description: Weekly pocket money tracking cycle
payload.justification: SG922 explicitly identifies weekly cycles as the temporal scope for pocket money tracking.

payload.description: Week conclusion triggering earnings presentation
payload.justification: SG926 specifies week conclusion as the business event when earnings aggregation is presented.

payload.description: Weekly reset event renewing task availability
payload.justification: SG928 establishes weekly reset as a recurring business event governing task lifecycle.

payload.description: Child role claiming and completing tasks
payload.justification: Multiple signals establish child as a distinct business role with specific task-related responsibilities.

payload.description: Parent role defining tasks and reviewing earnings
payload.justification: SG923 and SG927 define parent as a business role with task creation and payment oversight duties.

payload.description: Enable pocket money tracking through task completion
payload.justification: SG922 identifies the business objective of tracking pocket money earned via task claims.

payload.description: Support parent payment fulfillment through earnings visibility
payload.justification: SG927 establishes the business goal of enabling parents to fulfill payment obligations via reporting.

payload.description: Task viewing restricted to current week scope
payload.justification: SG925 implies a business constraint limiting child task visibility to the current weekly period.

payload.description: Historical task completion records must be preserved
payload.justification: SG929 establishes the business principle of maintaining persistent historical data for accountability.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Participation Structure
payload.description: Defines the enterprise's fundamental participant roles and their accountabilities within the family economy, establishing who has authority to commit resources and who is entitled to earn.

payload.name: Work-Value Exchange Obligation
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to a transparent exchange mechanism linking effort to compensation, including what constitutes compensable work and the terms of reward fulfillment.

payload.name: Earnings Transparency
payload.description: Governs the enterprise's obligation to provide clear, timely visibility into compensation status and performance outcomes to maintain trust and enable informed participation.

payload.name: Historical Accountability
payload.description: Defines the enterprise's responsibility to retain and make available complete records of work performed and compensation delivered, ensuring answerability and supporting retrospective analysis.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, maintaining, and governing the lifecycle of compensated household tasks, including their monetary values, availability windows, and renewal obligations.

payload.name: Completion Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing accountability when children claim and complete compensated tasks, preserving completion records, and maintaining historical evidence of task fulfillment obligations.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and presenting the monetary entitlement earned by children through task completion, enabling parent fulfillment of payment obligations within defined cycles.

payload.name: Stakeholder Participation
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing the distinct roles and participation boundaries of parent and child stakeholders within the pocket money obligation framework.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim completed tasks and receive pocket money.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to support child participation as task claimants and earners within the family economy structure.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define tasks and assign monetary rewards.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to support parental authority to commit resources through task definition and reward assignment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise tasks as units of work assigned to children.
payload.rationale: Establishes the fundamental entity that represents compensable work within the enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise monetary rewards associated with task completion.
payload.rationale: Defines the compensation entity that represents the value exchange for completed work.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain task completion status relationships.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural relationship linking tasks to their completion state, enabling claim eligibility determination.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define tasks and assign monetary rewards.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide the capability for configuring work-value exchanges.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to review earnings and tasks for payment fulfillment.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to provide oversight capability necessary for completing the value exchange cycle.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise task completion events as triggers for claim eligibility.
payload.rationale: Establishes the temporal commitment governing when rewards become claimable, forming a critical part of the work-value exchange mechanism.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to earn pocket money through task completion.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's fundamental strategic commitment and governance principle for the work-value exchange.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise weekly earnings as an aggregated compensation record.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural nature of weekly earnings as an entity composed of aggregated compensation data at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility of available and completed tasks to children.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable transparency of task status for participants.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide weekly earnings visibility to children.
payload.rationale: Enterprise obligation to deliver earnings transparency on a weekly cycle, combining the visibility commitment with its temporal boundary.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings visibility is updated and accessible on a weekly basis.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable motivational feedback through visible earnings.
payload.rationale: Strategic constraint governing the purpose of earnings visibility — to motivate children through transparent feedback mechanisms.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records of tasks and earnings.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's obligation to maintain the historical record entity, comprising tasks and earnings data.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure accountability through retention of historical records.
payload.rationale: Why-column governance obligation requiring the enterprise to retain records as a mechanism for ensuring accountability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable trend analysis through retention of historical data.
payload.rationale: Functional capability obligation to support trend analysis by retaining historical data; combines How-column retention function with Why-column analytical goal.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain task records with associated monetary values.
payload.rationale: Structural obligation describing the Task entity and its required monetary value attribute. What-column CCIs asserting entity composition and attributes.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly task lifecycle reset that renews task availability.
payload.rationale: Business-subject obligation for the recurring weekly reset cycle. How-column business rule and When-column event expressing a single behind-the-boundary responsibility for lifecycle governance.

payload.statement: The business shall restrict task viewing to the current week scope.
payload.rationale: Why-column constraint on task visibility enforcing temporal scope boundaries as a business governance rule.

payload.statement: The system shall make completed tasks claimable by entitled children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables children to claim completed tasks at the boundary. Complementary to the actor-capability requirement.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task completion.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the responsibility to maintain completion status and child-task completion relationships as business records behind the boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall preserve historical task completion records.
payload.rationale: Business governance constraint derived from Why-column principle and How-column retention rule requiring historical completion data to be retained.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings aggregate for each child.
payload.rationale: What-column artefact expressing the business responsibility to maintain the earnings aggregate entity.

payload.statement: The system shall make weekly earnings visible to the child.
payload.rationale: How-column capability expressing the system affordance of presenting earnings at the boundary to the child actor.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly pocket money tracking cycle.
payload.rationale: When-column cycle expressing the business responsibility to govern the temporal boundary of the tracking cycle, supporting the Why-column goal of enabling pocket money tracking.

payload.statement: The business shall present earnings to the child at week conclusion.
payload.rationale: When-column event triggering the How-column capability — the business is responsible for the timing and execution of earnings presentation behind the boundary.

payload.statement: A child shall be recognised as a business stakeholder with the capability to claim and complete tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the child as an external actor interacting with the system boundary, with defined participation capabilities in task claiming and completion.

payload.statement: A parent shall be recognised as a business stakeholder with the capability to define tasks and review earnings.
payload.rationale: Establishes the parent as an external actor interacting with the system boundary, with defined participation capabilities in task definition and earnings oversight.

payload.statement: A parent can define a task with a monetary value.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-How-002 (How/Function) and CCI-ROW2-C-What-007 (What/Relationship) express a single unified obligation: the capability for a parent to define tasks with associated monetary values. The How-column CCI describes the capability declaration; the What-column CCI describes the same parent action from a relationship perspective. Both belong to domain D005 (Task Stewardship) and describe the same boundary-crossing actor action. Subject = ACTOR (parent), per the boundary test: the parent reaches into the system to perform this action.

payload.statement: A parent can view their child's available tasks, completed tasks, earnings, and completion reports.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-How-004 (How/Function) expresses a single unified viewing capability across multiple related information types (available tasks, completed tasks, earnings, completion reports). The single obligation is the parent's capability to view this information. Subject = ACTOR (parent), per the boundary test: the parent reaches into the system to view information. All elements are viewing the same child's data, constituting one access obligation rather than four separate ones.

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings visible to the parent to support payment fulfillment.
payload.rationale: CCI-ROW2-C-Why-002 (Why/Goal) expresses the business motivation for earnings visibility: enabling parent payment fulfillment. Although Why-column, the content describes a system affordance (making earnings visible) to achieve a goal, not a governance constraint. The statement names the WHAT (visibility capability) without the HOW (technical realization). Subject = SYSTEM, per the boundary test: the system provides the capability at the boundary. This complements the parent's viewing capability in CCI-ROW2-C-How-004 but voices the system-side affordance motivated by payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose enabling children to claim completed tasks for pocket money throughout the week.
Describes parental capability intent to define tasks and associated monetary rewards.
Articulates visibility intent for children to view available and completed tasks.
Defines purpose of weekly earning visibility for children as motivational feedback.
Articulates parental oversight purpose to review earnings and tasks for payment fulfillment.
Expresses purpose for historical record retention supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Identifies the document section as providing a conceptual overview of the system.
Defines the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for pocket money tracking over weekly cycles.
Establishes the business entity relationship where parents define tasks with associated monetary values.
Specifies the conceptual association linking completed tasks to the child entity who performed them.
Defines child stakeholder capability to view both available and personally completed tasks within the current week.
Specifies the business concept of weekly earnings aggregation presented to child stakeholders at week conclusion.
Defines parent stakeholder capability to access child-specific earnings and task completion reports for payment fulfillment.
Establishes the business rule governing weekly task lifecycle reset and availability renewal.
Specifies the conceptual requirement for persistent retention of historical task completion data.
